q: Near which French town did a pivotal battle occur in 486 CE, where Clovis I defeated the last Roman governor of Gaul?
e: In 486 CE, Frankish king Clovis I defeated Syagrius, the last Roman ruler of Gaul, near Soissons. This victory gave Clovis control of the region between the Loire and Somme rivers, the core of the future Frankish kingdom.
i: Fixed: distractors were years (476, 488, 490, 480, 492, 475), not places; replaced with plausible French/Frankish cities to match the 'where' question format

q: Where did the Battle of the Persian Gate take place, in which Alexander the Great broke through a Persian defensive position to advance on Persepolis?
a: Persian Gates (mountain pass in the Zagros Mountains, modern Iran)
e: The Battle of the Persian Gate (330 BCE) was fought at a mountain pass in the Zagros Mountains near modern Yasuj, Iran. After an initial repulse, Alexander outflanked the Persian position and captured the pass, opening the road to Persepolis and its vast treasuries.
i: Fixed vague question (missing verb in original: 'Where did the Battle...?'); expanded answer to be informative; fixed distractors (Cappadocia, Pontus etc. were already in set; added Bactria and Sogdiana as plausible Alexander-related alternatives)
